Item 2. Management's Discussion and Analysis of Financial Condition and Results of Operations

Net sales increased 10 percent year-over-year during the fourth quarter, with holiday season results broadly in line with expectations and third-party seller services continuing to grow as a share of total unit sales.

Amazon Web Services revenue grew 21 percent year-over-year in the fourth quarter, reflecting accelerating enterprise commitment to cloud and artificial intelligence workloads on our platform.

Advertising services revenue grew 19 percent year-over-year, easing from the third quarter rate as comparables strengthened, though underlying demand from brand and performance advertisers remained robust.

International segment turned to operating profitability for the first time, driven by mature market performance improvements in Europe and efficiency gains across our global fulfillment and transportation network.

We launched Trainium and Inferentia chip-powered instances in AWS to provide customers with purpose-built, cost-effective compute options for training and deploying large-scale machine learning models.

Item 3. Other Information
